questions.prompt: How does TCP differ from UDP?

answers.text: TCP provides an ordered reliable byte stream.

feedback.legit: Correct. TCP provides an ordered reliable byte stream.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. TCP provides an ordered reliable byte stream.

answers.text: UDP datagrams can be lost, duplicated, or reordered.

feedback.legit: Correct. UDP datagrams can be lost, duplicated, or reordered.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. UDP datagrams can be lost, duplicated, or reordered.

answers.text: UDP avoids connection setup and lets applications choose their own reliability strategy.

feedback.legit: Correct. UDP avoids connection setup and lets applications choose their own reliability strategy.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. UDP avoids connection setup and lets applications choose their own reliability strategy.

answers.text: It encrypts every application connection automatically.

feedback.legit: Not quite. DNS resolution and transport encryption are separate mechanisms.
feedback.sus: Correct. DNS resolution and transport encryption are separate mechanisms.

answers.text: Resolvers may cache answers until their time-to-live expires.

feedback.legit: Correct. Resolvers may cache answers until their time-to-live expires.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Resolvers may cache answers until their time-to-live expires.

answers.text: Names such as hostnames into records including IP addresses.

feedback.legit: Correct. Names such as hostnames into records including IP addresses.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Names such as hostnames into records including IP addresses.

questions.prompt: What does idempotent mean for an HTTP method?

answers.text: An idempotent request must return byte-for-byte identical responses every time.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Idempotency concerns the intended server effect; response content can change between requests.
feedback.sus: Correct. Idempotency concerns the intended server effect; response content can change between requests.

answers.text: Repeating the same request has the same intended effect as sending it once.

feedback.legit: Correct. Repeating the same request has the same intended effect as sending it once.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Repeating the same request has the same intended effect as sending it once.

answers.text: The request has no side effects.

feedback.legit: Not quite. An idempotent request can change state; repetition should not add further intended change.
feedback.sus: Correct. An idempotent request can change state; repetition should not add further intended change.

questions.prompt: What does a TLS server certificate help a client verify?

answers.text: That a trusted issuer bound a public key to the requested identity.

feedback.legit: Correct. That a trusted issuer bound a public key to the requested identity.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. That a trusted issuer bound a public key to the requested identity.

answers.text: Certificates authenticate identity and keys, not application correctness.

feedback.legit: Correct. Certificates authenticate identity and keys, not application correctness.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Certificates authenticate identity and keys, not application correctness.

answers.text: The hostname and validity period are part of normal verification.

feedback.legit: Correct. The hostname and validity period are part of normal verification.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. The hostname and validity period are part of normal verification.

questions.prompt: What does network address translation commonly do?

answers.text: Provide end-to-end encryption by itself.

feedback.legit: Not quite. NAT changes addressing; encryption requires a protocol such as TLS or IPsec.
feedback.sus: Correct. NAT changes addressing; encryption requires a protocol such as TLS or IPsec.

answers.text: NAT is a complete firewall that makes separate traffic policy unnecessary.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Address translation is not a substitute for explicit firewall and access-control rules.
feedback.sus: Correct. Address translation is not a substitute for explicit firewall and access-control rules.

answers.text: Rewrite address and often port information between network realms.

feedback.legit: Correct. Rewrite address and often port information between network realms.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Rewrite address and often port information between network realms.

questions.prompt: What does an IP subnet prefix length describe?

answers.text: A host can compare the destination with its on-link prefixes when making a routing decision.

feedback.legit: Correct. A host can compare the destination with its on-link prefixes when making a routing decision.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. A host can compare the destination with its on-link prefixes when making a routing decision.

answers.text: Which leading address bits identify the network prefix.

feedback.legit: Correct. Which leading address bits identify the network prefix.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Which leading address bits identify the network prefix.

answers.text: The maximum TCP port number.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Ports belong to the transport layer and are unrelated to the subnet prefix.
feedback.sus: Correct. Ports belong to the transport layer and are unrelated to the subnet prefix.

questions.prompt: What does ARP do on an IPv4 local network?

answers.text: Map an IPv4 address to a link-layer address.

feedback.legit: Correct. Map an IPv4 address to a link-layer address.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Map an IPv4 address to a link-layer address.

answers.text: Resolve public domain names globally.

feedback.legit: Not quite. DNS resolves names; ARP operates on a local link.
feedback.sus: Correct. DNS resolves names; ARP operates on a local link.

answers.text: ARP requests are normally broadcast on the local network segment.

feedback.legit: Correct. ARP requests are normally broadcast on the local network segment.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. ARP requests are normally broadcast on the local network segment.

questions.prompt: How do flow control and congestion control differ?

answers.text: Congestion control reacts to capacity and contention in the network.

feedback.legit: Correct. Congestion control reacts to capacity and contention in the network.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Congestion control reacts to capacity and contention in the network.

answers.text: They are two names for the same receiver buffer setting.

feedback.legit: Not quite. They address different bottlenecks even though both can limit sending rate.
feedback.sus: Correct. They address different bottlenecks even though both can limit sending rate.

answers.text: Flow control protects the receiver from being overwhelmed.

feedback.legit: Correct. Flow control protects the receiver from being overwhelmed.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Flow control protects the receiver from being overwhelmed.

questions.prompt: What does browser CORS enforcement control?

answers.text: CORS by itself prevents cross-site request forgery against the server.

feedback.legit: Not quite. CORS mainly controls browser access to responses; CSRF needs its own defenses.
feedback.sus: Correct. CORS mainly controls browser access to responses; CSRF needs its own defenses.

answers.text: Whether frontend code may read certain cross-origin responses.

feedback.legit: Correct. Whether frontend code may read certain cross-origin responses.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Whether frontend code may read certain cross-origin responses.

answers.text: Whether a non-browser client can send packets to the server.

feedback.legit: Not quite. CORS is a browser security policy, not a network firewall.
feedback.sus: Correct. CORS is a browser security policy, not a network firewall.

questions.prompt: Why does QUIC run over UDP?

answers.text: It can implement transport features in user space while using UDP datagrams for network traversal.

feedback.legit: Correct. It can implement transport features in user space while using UDP datagrams for network traversal.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. It can implement transport features in user space while using UDP datagrams for network traversal.

answers.text: It gives up reliable ordered streams entirely.

feedback.legit: Not quite. QUIC implements reliability and multiple streams above UDP.
feedback.sus: Correct. QUIC implements reliability and multiple streams above UDP.

answers.text: Independent streams reduce transport-level head-of-line blocking between streams.

feedback.legit: Correct. Independent streams reduce transport-level head-of-line blocking between streams.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Independent streams reduce transport-level head-of-line blocking between streams.

questions.prompt: What changes after an HTTP connection is upgraded to WebSocket?

answers.text: Every message becomes an independent HTTP request.

feedback.legit: Not quite. WebSocket uses its own framing after the handshake.
feedback.sus: Correct. WebSocket uses its own framing after the handshake.

answers.text: The long-lived connection still needs failure detection and reconnection logic.

feedback.legit: Correct. The long-lived connection still needs failure detection and reconnection logic.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. The long-lived connection still needs failure detection and reconnection logic.

answers.text: Client and server can exchange framed messages in both directions.

feedback.legit: Correct. Client and server can exchange framed messages in both directions.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Client and server can exchange framed messages in both directions.

questions.prompt: What problem does path MTU discovery address?

answers.text: Path MTU discovery matters only for IPv4 and never for IPv6.

feedback.legit: Not quite. IPv6 also relies on path MTU discovery because routers do not fragment packets in transit.
feedback.sus: Correct. IPv6 also relies on path MTU discovery because routers do not fragment packets in transit.

answers.text: The largest MTU on any link is automatically safe for the whole path.

feedback.legit: Not quite. The path MTU is constrained by the smallest supported MTU along the route.
feedback.sus: Correct. The path MTU is constrained by the smallest supported MTU along the route.

answers.text: Choosing the fastest DNS resolver.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Path MTU concerns packet size, not name resolution.
feedback.sus: Correct. Path MTU concerns packet size, not name resolution.
